content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be improved by mimicking the selective gating mechanism of prefrontal-hippocampal interaction during memory retrieval, where only high-uncertainty task-relevant memory traces are prioritized for active querying. This biological interaction framework naturally solves the open problem of curiosity drift in modern curiosity-driven active learning agents by aligning memory retrieval with task-specific uncertainty, rather than relying on generic intrinsic reward signals like ICM or RND.
